Resolution¶

Contract inheritance resolution and deep merge utilities.

ContractResolver¶

Resolves contract inheritance chains.

from griot_core.resolution import ContractResolver, InMemoryFetcher

# Create resolver with fetcher
fetcher = InMemoryFetcher()
fetcher.register(parent_contract)

resolver = ContractResolver(fetcher)

# Resolve inheritance
resolved = resolver.resolve(child_contract)

# Access resolved definition
print(resolved.resolved_definition)  # Merged result
print(resolved.override_definition)  # Child's input
print(resolved.inheritance_chain)    # Parent URIs

Deep Merge¶

from griot_core.resolution import deep_merge

parent = {"a": 1, "b": {"c": 2}}
child = {"b": {"d": 3}}

merged = deep_merge(parent, child)
# {"a": 1, "b": {"c": 2, "d": 3}}

API Reference¶

Griot Core Resolution Module.

This module provides contract inheritance resolution, merging parent contracts with child overrides.

class griot_core.resolution.ContractResolver(fetcher=None, default_base=None, max_depth=10)[source]¶

Resolves contract inheritance chains and produces fully-merged contracts.

The resolver fetches parent contracts and merges them with child overrides according to the inheritance rules.

Resolution order (later overrides earlier): 1. griot://templates/base-contract (implicit root) 2. Parent contracts (recursively resolved) 3. This contract’s overrides

Example

>>> resolver = ContractResolver(registry_client)
>>> resolved = resolver.resolve(contract_dict)
>>> print(resolved.inheritance_chain)
['griot://templates/base-contract@1.0', 'griot://templates/pii-contract@1.0']
Parameters:

  • fetcher (Optional[ContractFetcher])

  • default_base (Optional[str])

  • max_depth (int)

DEFAULT_BASE = 'griot://templates/base-contract@1.0'¶
__init__(fetcher=None, default_base=None, max_depth=10)[source]¶

Initialize the resolver.

Parameters:

  • fetcher (ContractFetcher | None) – Contract fetcher for retrieving parent contracts

  • default_base (str | None) – Override the default base contract URI

  • max_depth (int) – Maximum inheritance depth to prevent runaway chains

clear_cache()[source]¶

Clear the resolution cache.

Return type:

None

resolve(contract, skip_base=False)[source]¶

Resolve a contract by merging with its parent chain.

Parameters:

  • contract (Dict[str, Any]) – The contract dictionary to resolve

  • skip_base (bool) – If True, don’t implicitly extend base contract

Returns:

  • resolved_definition: The fully merged contract

  • override_definition: The original contract

  • inheritance_chain: List of parent URIs

Return type:

ResolvedContract containing

Raises:

  • ContractNotFoundError – If a parent contract cannot be found

  • CircularInheritanceError – If inheritance chain has cycles

resolve_contract_object(contract, skip_base=False)[source]¶

Resolve a Contract object by merging with its parent chain.

This is a convenience method that converts the Contract to dict, resolves it, and returns the result.

Parameters:

  • contract (Contract) – The Contract object to resolve

  • skip_base (bool) – If True, don’t implicitly extend base contract

Returns:

ResolvedContract with resolved definition as dictionary

Return type:

ResolvedContract

set_fetcher(fetcher)[source]¶

Set the contract fetcher.

Parameters:

fetcher (ContractFetcher)

Return type:

None

class griot_core.resolution.ResolvedContract(resolved_definition, override_definition, inheritance_chain=<factory>, resolution_warnings=<factory>)[source]¶

Result of resolving a contract’s inheritance chain.

Parameters:

  • resolved_definition (Dict[str, Any])

  • override_definition (Dict[str, Any])

  • inheritance_chain (List[str])

  • resolution_warnings (List[str])

resolved_definition¶

The fully merged contract (what runs)

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

override_definition¶

The original contract (what user wrote)

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

inheritance_chain¶

List of parent contract URIs in order

Type:

List[str]

resolution_warnings¶

Any warnings encountered during resolution

Type:

List[str]

resolved_definition: Dict[str, Any]¶
override_definition: Dict[str, Any]¶
inheritance_chain: List[str]¶
resolution_warnings: List[str]¶
class griot_core.resolution.InMemoryFetcher(contracts=None)[source]¶

Simple in-memory contract fetcher for testing.

Stores contracts in a dictionary keyed by URI.

Parameters:

contracts (Optional[Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]])

add(uri, contract)[source]¶

Add a contract to the store.

Parameters:

  • uri (str)

  • contract (Dict[str, Any])

Return type:

None

fetch(uri)[source]¶

Fetch a contract by URI.

Parameters:

uri (str)

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

remove(uri)[source]¶

Remove a contract from the store.

Parameters:

uri (str)

Return type:

None

exception griot_core.resolution.ContractNotFoundError(uri, message='')[source]¶

Raised when a contract cannot be found.

Parameters:

  • uri (str)

  • message (str)

exception griot_core.resolution.CircularInheritanceError(chain)[source]¶

Raised when a circular inheritance chain is detected.

Parameters:

chain (List[str])

griot_core.resolution.deep_merge(parent, child, list_strategy='extend')[source]¶

Deep merge two dictionaries with child values overriding parent.

Special handling: - Scalars: Child replaces parent - Dicts: Recursively merged (child keys override parent) - Lists: Strategy-based (extend, replace, or merge by key)

Parameters:

  • parent (Dict[str, Any]) – The parent/base dictionary

  • child (Dict[str, Any]) – The child/override dictionary

  • list_strategy (str) – How to handle lists: - “extend”: Child list extends parent list - “replace”: Child list replaces parent list - “merge”: Lists of dicts merged by ‘name’ or ‘id’ key

Returns:

Merged dictionary with child overrides applied

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]
